Container.Name: OpenClaw
Container.Overview: OpenClaw is a self-hosted AI assistant gateway that connects Claude, GPT, Gemini, Groq, and any OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible LLM router to messaging platforms like Discord, Telegram, WhatsApp, Slack, and more. Features file access, shell commands, browser control, cron jobs, and a web-based Control UI. === REQUIRED ON FIRST INSTALL === 1. Gateway Token (any secret -- generate with: openssl rand -hex 24) 2. Allowed Origins -- your Unraid host URL with the Control UI port, e.g. http://192.168.1.41:18789. Comma-separated for multiple. WITHOUT THIS THE GATEWAY REFUSES TO START since OpenClaw 2026.2. 3. ONE LLM source -- either a built-in provider key (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Groq, OpenRouter, xAI, Z.AI) OR the Custom LLM trio (Base URL + API Key + API Type) for your own router (LiteLLM, vLLM, Ollama, etc.) === USER / GROUP (PUID/PGID) === The gateway runs under the UID/GID you set in PUID/PGID (defaults: 99:100 = nobody:users on Unraid). Files in your appdata mounts will be owned by these IDs, so SMB/NFS clients see them with the right ownership automatically -- no manual chown loops, no permission fixes. To use a different user: set PUID/PGID to your host user's id (run `id $USER` on the Unraid console). The bootstrap will align ownership of mount points to PUID:PGID on first start; subsequent starts are zero-cost. === POST-INSTALL STEPS === 1. Open Control UI: http://YOUR-UNRAID-IP:18789/?token=YOUR-GATEWAY-TOKEN (the ?token= part is mandatory). 2. If you used a non-Anthropic provider OR the Custom LLM endpoint, change the default model: Config tab -> Agents -> Raw JSON -> set agents.defaults.model.primary to match your provider (e.g. google/gemini-2.0-flash, openai/gpt-4o, custom/your-model-id). Save and restart the container. 3. (Optional) Connect a messaging channel: Config tab -> Channels. Or set Discord/Telegram bot tokens in this template, then approve pairing in the Agents tab on first message. 4. (Optional) Install Homebrew if a skill needs go/npm tools -- open the container console and run: NONINTERACTIVE=1 /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)" Review brew.sh before running. The template already configures PATH. === DO NOT USE === CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN / claude setup-token: Anthropic prohibits using Claude Code subscription tokens with third-party tooling. Use a real API key from console.anthropic.com instead. === TROUBLESHOOTING === "origin not allowed" -> Allowed Origins value must match the URL in your browser EXACTLY (scheme + host + port). Edit, Apply, restart. "No API key found for provider 'anthropic'" -> You used a non-Anthropic key without changing the default model. See post-install step 2. Container goes to STOP after gateway restart -> should not happen with default --restart=unless-stopped. If your Restart Policy got overridden to 'no', set it back to Unless Stopped. Files invisible via SMB -> verify PUID/PGID match your host user (run `id $USER`). If they differ, update the template fields and restart. Container slow / gateway stalls -> if upgrading from v1.1.0 or earlier, the legacy chown loop may still be running. Update the template (Apply) to pick up the v1.1.1+ bootstrap which removes it. Full guide: https://github.com/thebtf/openclaw-unraid OpenClaw project: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw OpenClaw docs: https://docs.openclaw.ai
Container.Description: OpenClaw is a self-hosted AI assistant gateway. Access the Control UI at http://YOUR-UNRAID-IP:18789/?token=YOUR-GATEWAY-TOKEN. Requires Gateway Token, Allowed Origins, and one LLM API key or custom LLM endpoint.

Config.Name: Control UI Port
Config.Description: Port for Control UI and Gateway API. Access at http://IP:18789/?token=YOUR_TOKEN

Config.Name: Config Path
Config.Description: Path to OpenClaw config, sessions, credentials. Mounted at /home/node/.openclaw -- canonical $HOME/.openclaw for the node user that runs the gateway. (Pre-v1.1.4 templates used /root/.openclaw; if you upgraded, the same host appdata folder is now seen at the new container path.)

Config.Name: Workspace Path
Config.Description: Path for agent workspace (files, memory, AGENTS.md, projects)

Config.Name: Projects Path
Config.Description: Optional additional path for coding projects

Config.Name: Homebrew Path
Config.Description: Persistent storage for Homebrew packages. If you install Homebrew manually via console, this mount ensures it persists across restarts.

Config.Name: Local Tools Path
Config.Description: Persistent storage for ~/.local (pip --user, manually-built CLIs in ~/.local/bin). Mounted at /home/node/.local -- canonical XDG location for the node user. PATH already includes /home/node/.local/bin.

Config.Name: Logs Path
Config.Description: OpenClaw gateway log files. Bootstrap pins logging.file=/tmp/openclaw/openclaw.log so logs land here on the host volume regardless of OpenClaw's instance namespacing (since 2026.4 the default is /tmp/openclaw-0/). Built-in rotation: 100 MB per file (configurable via Log Max File Bytes), 5 numbered archives kept (hardcoded in openclaw).

Config.Name: Gateway Token
Config.Description: Secret token for authentication. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 24 (or use any secret value). Required to access the Control UI.

Config.Name: Allowed Origins
Config.Description: REQUIRED. Browser origins allowed to connect to the Control UI. Comma-separated list of full URLs (scheme + host + port). Example: http://192.168.1.41:18789 or http://192.168.1.41:18789,http://openclaw.local:18789. Without this the gateway refuses to start (OpenClaw 2026.2+ enforces this on non-loopback hosts). Use your Unraid IP and the Control UI Port above.

Config.Name: Disable Device Auth
Config.Description: Disable browser device-identity check. Default 'true' for LAN/homelab use over plain HTTP -- without this you get 'control ui requires device identity (use HTTPS or localhost secure context)' because Web Crypto API needs a secure context (HTTPS or http://localhost). Token auth is still required regardless. Set to 'false' if you access the Control UI via a real HTTPS reverse proxy and want full device-identity protection.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: Host user ID the gateway runs under. Default 99 = nobody on Unraid. Set to your host user's UID (run `id $USER` on Unraid console) so files in appdata are owned by you and visible via SMB/NFS. The bootstrap aligns mount-point ownership to PUID:PGID once at startup, then exec's the gateway under those IDs -- no chown loops, no permission fix loops.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Host group ID the gateway runs under. Default 100 = users on Unraid. Set to your host user's GID (`id $USER`).

Config.Name: Log Max File Bytes
Config.Description: (Optional) Max bytes per log file before rotation. Default 104857600 (100 MB) -- matches OpenClaw upstream. Total disk = ~6 * this value (5 archives + active) = ~600 MB at default. Logs go to /tmp/openclaw/openclaw.log (pinned via logging.file).

Config.Name: Skip Ownership Init
Config.Description: (Optional) Set to 1 to skip the one-shot ownership alignment at container start. Bootstrap normally aligns mount ownership to PUID:PGID once, then exec's the gateway under those IDs (no loops, no recursive chown on a tick). Disable only if you manage ownership externally (e.g. via Unraid User Scripts).

Config.Name: Custom LLM Reasoning
Config.Description: (Optional) Whether the custom LLM model(s) support reasoning/thinking blocks. Default 'true' for modern reasoning models (gpt-5.5, o1, claude-opus-4.7). Set 'false' for non-reasoning models (llama, gpt-4o-mini) -- without this OpenClaw won't surface reasoning tokens correctly.

Config.Name: Custom LLM Base URL
Config.Description: (Optional) Base URL for a custom LLM endpoint. OpenAI-compatible: http://litellm:4000/v1 or http://ollama:11434/v1. Anthropic-compatible: https://my-anthropic-router.example.com. Leave empty if using built-in providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.) below.

Config.Name: Custom LLM API Key
Config.Description: (Optional) API key for the custom LLM endpoint above. Use 'ollama' for local Ollama, your router token otherwise. Required if Custom LLM Base URL is set.

Config.Name: Custom LLM API Type
Config.Description: (Optional) Protocol adapter for the custom endpoint. THIS IS NOT THE MODEL NAME -- put the model name in 'Custom LLM Model ID' below. Allowed values: openai-completions (LiteLLM, vLLM, Ollama, OpenRouter), openai-responses (new OpenAI Responses API), openai-codex-responses, anthropic-messages, google-generative-ai, github-copilot, bedrock-converse-stream, ollama, azure-openai-responses.

Config.Name: Custom LLM Model ID
Config.Description: (Optional) Model identifier(s) exposed by your custom endpoint. Required if Custom LLM Base URL is set. Examples: gpt-5.5, llama-3.1-70b, claude-3-opus. Comma-separated for multiple: gpt-5.5,claude-3-opus. The first model is pinned as agent primary (agents.list[0].model = custom/MODEL_ID) so plugin auto-enable can't silently swap it to a different namespace.

Config.Name: Custom LLM Context Window
Config.Description: (Optional) Total context window in tokens for the custom model(s). Look up your actual model's value -- e.g. gpt-5.5 = 1050000, gpt-4o = 128000, claude-opus-4.7 = 200000, gemini-2.0-flash = 1048576. Default 128000. If you have multiple models with different windows, edit Raw JSON post-install for per-model values.

Config.Name: Custom LLM Max Tokens
Config.Description: (Optional) Max output tokens per response for the custom model(s). E.g. gpt-5.5 = 128000, gpt-4o = 16384, claude-opus-4.7 = 8192. Default 32000. Adjust if your provider rejects with 'max_tokens too high'.

Config.Name: Anthropic API Key
Config.Description: API key for Claude models. Get from console.anthropic.com (separate from Claude Pro/Max subscription).

Config.Name: OpenAI API Key
Config.Description: API key for GPT models. Get from platform.openai.com

Config.Name: OpenRouter API Key
Config.Description: API key for OpenRouter - access 100+ models. Get from openrouter.ai

Config.Name: Gemini API Key
Config.Description: API key for Google Gemini models. Get from aistudio.google.com

Config.Name: Groq API Key
Config.Description: API key for Groq (fast Llama/Mixtral inference). Get from console.groq.com

Config.Name: xAI API Key
Config.Description: API key for xAI Grok models. Get from console.x.ai

Config.Name: Z.AI API Key
Config.Description: API key for Z.AI/Zhipu GLM models. Get from z.ai/model-api

Config.Name: GitHub Copilot Token
Config.Description: (Advanced) GitHub Copilot OAuth token. Requires running 'openclaw models auth login-github-copilot' on another machine first.

Config.Name: Discord Bot Token
Config.Description: (Optional) Discord bot token for Discord integration. Can also configure via Control UI after install.

Config.Name: Telegram Bot Token
Config.Description: (Optional) Telegram bot token from @BotFather. Can also configure via Control UI after install.

Config.Name: Gateway Port
Config.Description: Internal gateway port. Change if 18789 is already in use (must match Control UI Port).

Config.Name: PATH
Config.Description: System PATH including ~/.local/bin (persistent local tools), Homebrew, Bun, Cargo. Do not modify unless you know what you're doing.

Config.Name: Web Search API Key
Config.Description: (Optional) Brave Search API key for web search. Free tier: 2000 queries/month at brave.com/search/api
